join_order.notes: Build uint8_t green_part[2000001] by scanning part/p_name.bin; strstr for 'green'. ~5.5% selectivity → ~3.3M passing lineitem rows.

At startup, scan partsupp sequentially (ps_partkey.bin, ps_suppkey.bin, ps_supplycost.bin — 122MB sequential). Insert only rows where green_part[ps_partkey]==1 into a compact open-addressing hash table: 2^20=1,048,576 slots, each slot=(uint64_t key, double cost)=16 bytes, total 16MB → fits in 44MB L3. Key formula: partkey*200001ULL+suppkey (identical to prebuilt index). LOG2_CAP=20, FIB_CONST=11400714819323198485ULL, linear probe. Empty sentinel key=0ULL. Eliminates all LLC misses for partsupp lookup vs the prebuilt 256MB hash table.

Build int8_t okey_yearoff[60000001] at startup: mmap orders_by_orderkey.bin (240MB) + o_orderdate.bin (57MB); for each entry in orders_by_orderkey where value != -1, compute extract_year(o_orderdate[row]) - 1992 and store as int8_t at position [orderkey]. Entries with no order (sentinel -1) store value 127 (or 0xFF for out-of-range sentinel). Hot path: year_off = (int)okey_yearoff[l_orderkey[i]] — single 1-byte random access into 60MB vs two int32_t accesses into 297MB. Halves LLC misses for orders. Software prefetch: __builtin_prefetch(&okey_yearoff[l_orderkey[i+16]], 0, 1) in hot loop.
Precompute int32_t suppkey_to_nationrow[100001]: load supplier_by_suppkey + s_nationkey + nation_by_nationkey. 400KB, always L3-resident. Same as iter_0.
Load 25-row nation table (650 bytes). Fold into suppkey_to_nationrow precomputation.

data_structures.compact_partsupp_ht: open-addressing hash, 2^20 slots, slot=(uint64_t key, double cost)=16 bytes, 16MB total → L3-resident (44MB L3); built at runtime by sequential scan of partsupp 8M rows, inserting only green-part entries (~440K); replaces prebuilt 256MB partsupp_hash_index
data_structures.orderkey_yearoff: int8_t[60000001], 60MB, single-level direct array indexed by l_orderkey → year offset 0-6 (127 for missing); built at startup from orders_by_orderkey+o_orderdate; replaces two-level 297MB lookup
data_structures.aggregation: double profits[25][10] per thread (cache-line aligned struct), zero-initialized; reduced single-threaded after parallel phase

parallelism.parallel_phase: lineitem scan (60M rows split across 64 threads)
parallelism.thread_local_state: double profits[25][10] per thread, in alignas(64) struct to avoid false sharing
parallelism.reduction: single-threaded sum over thread_profits[t][n][y] after join phase completes

indexes.purpose: O(1) filter of lineitem rows by l_partkey; 2MB, L3-resident
O(1) (l_partkey, l_suppkey) → ps_supplycost with L3-resident hash table; replaces prebuilt 256MB partsupp_hash_index to eliminate LLC misses
O(1) l_orderkey → year_offset (0–6); single 1-byte access replaces two-level int32 dereference through 297MB orders structures
O(1) l_suppkey → nation row index; 400KB, L3-resident

optimization_notes: Root cause (Q2 — physical implementation vs hardware): the hot path's two dominant random-access structures both greatly exceed the 44MB LLC. The prebuilt partsupp_hash_index is 256MB (5.8× LLC) and the two-level orders lookup (orders_by_orderkey 240MB + o_orderdate 57MB = 297MB, 6.7× LLC) together produce ~9.9M LLC cache misses for the 3.3M qualifying lineitem rows. Strategy Category B: replace the prebuilt 256MB partsupp hash with a compact runtime-built open-addressing hash covering only the ~440K green-part partsupp entries (2^20 = 1M slots × 16 bytes = 16MB), which fits within the 44MB L3 and converts all partsupp lookups from LLC misses to L3 hits. Strategy Category C: replace the two-level orders dereference (two int32_t random accesses into 297MB) with a single int8_t okey_yearoff[60000001] = 60MB direct array built at startup, halving the orders cache miss count and reducing the per-access transfer from 8 bytes (two int32_t) to 1 byte. Together these reduce the combined hot-path random-access footprint from ~553MB to ~76MB (16MB L3-resident partsupp + 60MB orders), making partsupp lookups fully L3-resident and cutting orders miss frequency by ~50%.
